title: Synthetic Identity Established Through Commercial Mail Receiving Agency
narrative: A fraudster constructs a synthetic identity using a commercial mail receiving agency (CMRA) address, often identifiable by specific ZIP codes known for virtual mailbox services (e.g., 33166, 90001, 10036). The account shows a 30-45 day aging period with small-dollar purchases ($12-$47) at low-risk MCCs (grocery, pharmacy) to establish legitimacy. Initial purchases use free email domains with numeric suffixes (P_emaildomain: gmail.com, username pattern: firstname.lastname[4-digit]). After the aging period, transaction velocity spikes to 8-12 transactions in 72 hours with TransactionAmt jumping 400-800% above baseline. The dist1 feature shows consistent 0.0 distance between billing and shipping addresses initially, then shipping addresses (addr2) suddenly diverge to high-risk states while addr1 remains static. Card utilization accelerates from 15% to 95% within one billing cycle, with txn_hour clustering in business hours (9-17) during aging, then shifting to late evening (22-02) during bust-out phase.
indicators: addr1 matches known CMRA ZIP codes with dist1 = 0.0 for first 8-15 transactions
P_emaildomain is free provider with numeric suffix pattern in username
Initial 6-10 transactions under $50 at MCC 5411, 5912, 5122 over 30-45 days
TransactionAmt increases 400-800% after aging period with 8-12 txns in 72 hours
card1_amt_zscore jumps from -0.3 range to +2.5 range within one week
addr2 divergence from addr1 begins after day 35 with dist1 increasing to 500+ miles
txn_hour pattern shifts from daytime (9-17) to late night (22-02) during acceleration phase
summary: A newly created account uses a commercial mail receiving agency address and starts with small grocery or pharmacy purchases over 30-45 days, then suddenly escalates to 8-12 high-value transactions within 72 hours while shipping addresses diverge to different states and purchase times shift to late evening.
reasoning: The fraudster is building credibility with a fake identity by mimicking normal spending patterns, then executing a coordinated bust-out once the account appears legitimate, maximizing credit before the synthetic identity is detected and the account closed.
indicator_explanations: Shipping address is a commercial mail drop with zero distance variation early on
Email uses free provider with suspicious numbered username suggesting mass generation
Account starts with small grocery and drug store purchases to build legitimacy
Spending suddenly explodes with rapid-fire high-value transactions after waiting period
Transaction amounts spike dramatically from below-average to well above cardholder norm
Shipping destinations split geographically after initial trust-building phase concludes
Transaction timing switches from business hours to graveyard shift during cash-out
